eligibility.residency: Resident of the state where registering

votingMethods.name: In-Person Voting
votingMethods.description: Vote at your polling place on Election Day
steps: Locate your polling place at vote.gov or your state election website
Bring valid photo ID (varies by state)
Tell poll worker your name and address
Receive ballot and mark your choices
Feed ballot into scanner or give to poll worker

votingMethods.name: Early Voting
votingMethods.description: Vote before Election Day at designated early voting sites
steps: Check if your state offers early voting
Find early voting location and hours
Vote as you would on Election Day

votingMethods.name: Vote by Mail / Absentee
votingMethods.description: Request and return a mail-in ballot
steps: Request mail-in ballot from your county clerk
Return by mail or drop box before deadline

registrationSteps.title: Check Eligibility
registrationSteps.description: Must be 18 by Election Day, U.S. citizen, resident of state

registrationSteps.title: Register to Vote
registrationSteps.description: Register online at vote.gov or your state website. Deadline varies by state (7–30 days before election).

registrationSteps.title: Confirm Registration
registrationSteps.description: Verify your registration status at your state's voter portal

registrationSteps.title: Locate Polling Place
registrationSteps.description: Find your assigned polling location via vote.gov

registrationSteps.title: Know the Deadlines
registrationSteps.description: Registration deadline, early voting dates, mail ballot request deadline

registrationSteps.title: Cast Your Vote
registrationSteps.description: Vote in-person, early, or by mail on Election Day

timeline.label: Registration Opens
timeline.description: Voter registration available year-round in most states

timeline.label: Registration Deadline
timeline.description: Varies by state — 7 to 30 days before Election Day

timeline.label: Early Voting Begins
timeline.description: Most states open early voting 2–3 weeks before Election Day

timeline.label: Election Day
timeline.description: Presidential elections: First Tuesday after first Monday in November

timeline.label: Vote Counting
timeline.description: Ballots counted — mail ballots may take days

timeline.label: Inauguration
timeline.description: Winner takes oath of office (January 20 for President)

faqs.q: Can I vote without a photo ID?
faqs.a: Depends on your state. Some states have strict photo ID requirements, others accept non-photo ID or affidavits. Check your state's specific rules at vote.gov.

faqs.q: What if I miss the registration deadline?
faqs.a: Some states offer same-day voter registration at the polls. Check if your state allows this at vote.gov.
It depends on the state and offense. Some states restore voting rights after release, others after probation/parole. Maine and Vermont allow voting even while incarcerated.

faqs.q: How do I track my mail ballot?
faqs.a: Most states provide online ballot tracking through your county or state election website. ballottrax.net serves many counties.
